title: Medical Science Liaison
description:
**The position**


The MSL reports to the Country Medical Director. The MSL provides medical support, engages with Scientific Leaders (SLs) / Key Decision Makers (KDMs), and drives scientific initiatives.Therapeutic focus is bone, pain and dermatology.

**\*\*Please apply with your CV in English\*\***

**Responsibilities**

* Communicate scientific therapy needs credibly, appropriately, and effectively across a variety of internal and external stakeholders at all levels.
* Build trust with external scientific community via peer\-to\-peer scientific exchange, research and educational collaborations, interactions with scientific societies and health organizations, publications, etc.
* Help execute country medical educational programs and symposia.
* Support Medical advisors in conducting Advisory Boards and Expert Input Forums in line with strategy.
* Participate in and contribute significantly to professional organizations, academic or regulatory working teams.
* Provide scientific inputs for digital initiatives.

 **Required Education, Experience, and Skills**

* Excellent communication skills (verbal, written, presentations) and interpersonal skills.
* Knowledge of local regulatory and clinical research guidelines.
* Strong prioritization skills and ability to understand how decisions fit into the broader corporate strategies.
* Minimum 1\-2 years Medical Science Liaison experience (preferentially)
* Clinical and/or research experience in therapeutic area of interest (preferred)
* Medical degree, Pharm D or Pharmacist (Pharm D equivalent), Biologist with PhD

**Secondary Job Description**

**Who We Are:**

Organon delivers ingenious health solutions that enable people to live their best lives. We are a $6\.5 billion global healthcare company focused on making a world of difference for women, their families and the communities they care for. We have an important portfolio and are growing it by investing in the unmet needs of Women’s Health, expanding access to leading biosimilars and touching lives with a diverse and trusted portfolio of health solutions. Our Vision is clear: A better and healthier every day for every woman.


As an equal opportunity employer, we welcome applications from candidates with a diverse background. We are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all our applicants.

**Search Firm Representatives Please Read Carefully**  

Organon LLC, does not accept unsolicited assistance from search firms for employment opportunities. All CVs / resumes submitted by search firms to any employee at our company without a valid written search agreement in place for this position will be deemed the sole property of our company. No fee will be paid in the event a candidate is hired by our company as a result of an agency referral where no pre\-existing agreement is in place. Where agency agreements are in place, introductions are position specific. Please, no phone calls or emails.

**Annualized Salary Range**

**Annualized Salary Range (Global)**

**Annualized Salary Range (Canada)**

**Please Note: Pay ranges are specific to local market and therefore vary from country to country.**

**Employee Status:**

Regular**Relocation:**


Domestic**VISA Sponsorship:**

**Travel Requirements: Organon employees must be able to satisfy all applicable travel and credentialing requirements, including associated vaccination prerequisites**

**Flexible Work Arrangements:**

**Shift:**

**Valid Driving License:**

**Hazardous Material(s):**

**Number of Openings:**

title: New business Introduction lead, Ferentino, Italy
description:
**Work Schedule**


Standard (Mon\-Fri)**Environmental Conditions**


Office**Job Description**


As part of the Thermo Fisher Scientific team, you’ll discover meaningful work that makes a positive impact on a global scale. Join our colleagues in bringing our Mission to life every single day to enable our customers to make the world healthier, cleaner and safer. We provide our global teams with the resources needed to achieve individual career goals while helping to take science a step beyond by developing solutions for some of the world’s toughest challenges, like protecting the environment, making sure our food is safe or helping find cures for cancer.  

  

DESCRIPTION:  

Join our R\&D organization as a New Business Introduction Lead and contribute to innovation and excellence across Thermo Fisher Scientific's diverse portfolio.


The *New Business Introduction Lead* is responsible for defining, planning, and executing strategies for the introduction of new businesses, products, or services within the pharmaceutical sector. This role has a strong focus on evaluating new business opportunities, assessing business feasibility, and leading cross\-functional project evaluation.


This position works closely with internal Quotation, Regulatory Affairs, Quality, Manufacturing, Commercial teams, and external stakeholders to ensure successful, compliant, and profitable business introductions.


You will help bring innovative solutions to market that enable our customers to make the world healthier, cleaner and safer.

### **Key Responsibilities**

* Lead projects related to the introduction of new products, services, or business lines in the pharmaceutical market
* Identify and evaluate new business opportunities aligned with company strategy
* Coordinate cross\-functional teams to ensure new business evaluation in alignment with timelines, costs, and quality
* Manage the *New Business Introduction* process from concept through the proposal signature
* Collaborate with Regulatory Affairs to ensure compliance with applicable regulations (EMA, FDA, GMP, etc.)
* Interface with key clients and strategic partners to facilitate new business introduction and quotation
* Lead and develop quotation assessment in collaboration with cross\-functional team
* Foster a culture of innovation and continuous improvement
* Influence strategic decision\-making at the organizational level

  

Requirements:  

* Advanced Degree or Bachelor's Degree plus 5 years of experience in business management, project/program management, preferably in regulated industries like pharmaceuticals, medical devices or high\-tech manufacturing
* Preferred Fields of Study: Pharmacy, Chemistry, Biotechnology, Engineering) or business\-related field
* Project Management Professional (PMP) certification desired
* 5–10 years of experience in the pharmaceutical or life sciences industry
* Knowledge of project management methodologies, tools and best practices
* Proven experience in *New Business Introduction*, Business Development
* Excellent communication and presentation skills with ability to influence senior stakeholders
* Strong analytical and problem\-solving capabilities with data\-driven decision making
* Demonstrated leadership abilities in matrix organizations
* Strong ability to manage complex projects quotations
* Ability to manage multiple priorities and achieve results in a responsive environment
* Fluency in English required; additional languages beneficial
* Willingness to travel if needed
* Expertise in relevant technical domain (e.g. software development, manufacturing processes, validation) based on role focus
* Knowledge of regulatory requirements and quality management systems
* Strong interpersonal skills with ability to build relationships at all levels
* Experience with continuous improvement and process optimization
company_description: At Thermo Fisher Scientific, our professionals develop critical solutions—and build rewarding careers.

title: AI Team Lead Video Editor
description:
**CapsLock** is a dynamic marketing company providing IT\-enabled solutions for large North American partners. We specialize in scalable customer acquisition through data\-driven marketing, creative production, and technology innovation.


Our teams collaborate across marketing, IT, analytics, and media buying to build, test, and optimize high\-performing funnels that help clients achieve predictable growth across industries such as Home Improvement, Health, Legal, and Insurance.

**Role Overview**


We’re looking for an **AI Team Lead Video Editor** who is equal parts strong leader, project manager, systems builder, and editing geek — someone who knows how to automate workflows and achieve business goals through videos that actually sell.


You will take a team of 3 to 7 editors and elevate them through mature management, strict quality control, and deep AI integration.

 **Core Responsibilities**

**Team \& Project Management**

* Lead, mentor, and scale a team of 3 to 7\+ AI\-driven video editors, identifying their core strengths and guiding them to new professional heights.
* Monitor team morale and proactively manage workloads to prevent burnout, ensuring the team’s spirit aligns with the company culture.
* Act as a strong Project Manager: own the task distribution process and handle complex project routing. Keep pipelines organized and ensure clean, on\-time delivery.
* Ensure strict Quality Control (QC). Every video must meet our high standards for pacing, visual fidelity, and hook impact.
* Review, approve, and uphold internal guidelines and editing rules so the team has a clear operational framework.

 **AI Systems \& Workflow Automation**

* **Build systematized AI workflows.** Create structured templates using tools like Weavy to speed up the team's execution.
* **Automate post\-production.** Build pipelines connecting task databases to AI generators, auto\-delivering ready\-to\-edit assets directly, eliminating manual prompt copying and file downloading.
* **Integrate new AI tools.** Regularly test emerging AI models and implement the most effective ones into the daily workflow.
* **Stay hands\-on.** Jump into the editing software (Adobe Suite) when needed to unblock the team, fix complex issues, or test new technical approaches.

**Performance**

* Build and maintain a database of top\-performing creatives to systematically scale and iterate on winning concepts.
* Analyze failed creatives to understand what went wrong and make corrections for future videos.

**Cross\-Functional Collaboration**

* Work tightly with Creative Producers, Copywriters, Media Buyers, and Marketers to align on technical feasibility, negotiate deadlines, and solve bottlenecks.
* Actively participate in brainstorming sessions, pitching new visual formats and AI\-driven approaches to turn creative hypotheses into reality.

  

Requirements:  

  

**Requirements**

* **5\+ years of Leadership Experience:** You have successfully managed video production/editing teams. *(This is not a role for a senior editor looking for their first management gig).*
* **Mature Leader \& Problem Solver:** You build clear processes, guide your team, gracefully resolve conflicts, remain highly proactive, and stay completely open to new ideas and approaches.
* **Strong Project Manager:** You drive every project cleanly to the finish line. You guarantee top\-tier quality, maintain absolute order, and hit strict deadlines without excuses.
* **Editing Geek:** Deep, hands\-on expertise in the Adobe Creative Cloud suite (Premiere Pro, After Effects). You know the editing world inside out and can troubleshoot or solve any technical problem the team encounters.
* **AI \& Automation Geek:** This is far beyond basic ChatGPT prompting. You actively track the latest AI video tools and integrate them to automate manual workflows. You know exactly how to create realistic AI avatars with perfect lip\-sync, generate missing footage from scratch, anduse AI to solve any production problem.
* **Performance Marketing DNA:** Solid understanding of short\-form video ads (Meta, TikTok, YouTube Shorts). You know what keeps viewers watching and what makes a video sell.
* **Fluent English** — both spoken and written.

 **Why do we deserve your attention?**


Not only do we offer fair and competitive pay, but we also provide perks and benefits that support your well\-being and growth. Here are some of them:

 **Remote Work**


We are a truly remote\-first company. You choose where you feel the most productive and comfortable to make an impact.

 **Paid Time Off**


Work\-life balance matters to us, and we support it with paid time off for rest, personal time, or simply doing what makes you happy.

 **Ongoing Learning**


We believe in curiosity and growth. That’s why we support continuous learning with workshops, online courses, conferences, and certifications.

 **Home Office**


We help you set up your perfect workspace by covering equipment and furniture expenses, so you can do your best work from home.

 **Physical Well\-Being**


From gym memberships and yoga classes to home workout equipment or even a bicycle, we give you the flexibility to stay active your way.

 **Unlimited Sick Days**


Your health matters. Take the time you need to rest and recover \- we’ll be here when you’re ready to return.

 **Medical Coverage**


We support you and your family with reimbursement for medical insurance and health\-related expenses, including dental care and psychological support.

 **Co\-Working Space**


If you sometimes crave a change of scenery or more social interaction, we’ll cover the full cost of a co\-working space.

 **Fun Stuff**


From gaming nights and fitness challenges to annual retreats at world\-class resorts, we make sure there’s never a shortage of fun and connection.

title: Product Specialist Biosurgery | Marche e Umbria
description:
At Johnson \& Johnson, we believe health is everything. Our strength in healthcare innovation empowers us to build a world where complex diseases are prevented, treated, and cured, where treatments are smarter and less invasive, and solutions are personal. Through our expertise in Innovative Medicine and MedTech, we are uniquely positioned to innovate across the full spectrum of healthcare solutions today to deliver the breakthroughs of tomorrow, and profoundly impact health for humanity. Learn more at jnj.com.


As guided by Our Credo, Johnson \& Johnson is responsible to our employees who work with us throughout the world. We provide an inclusive work environment where each person is considered as an individual. At Johnson \& Johnson, we respect the diversity and dignity of our employees and recognize their merit.

**Job Function:**

MedTech Sales**Job Sub Function:**

Clinical Sales – Surgeons (Commission)**Job Category:**


Business Enablement/Support**All Job Posting Locations:**


Pomezia, Roma, Italy**Job Description:**

**A proposito di Chirurgia**


Ispirati dall'innovazione che nasce dall’incontro tra biologia e tecnologia, stiamo sviluppando la prossima generazione di cure innovative, meno invasive e più personalizzate.


Il tuo obiettivo è fornire il tuo contributo nel migliorare ed ampliare le opportunità dell'Ortopedia? Sei pronto a entrare a far parte di un team che sta reinventando il modo in cui curiamo i nostri pazienti? I nostri team di Ortopedia aiutano ogni anno più di 6 milioni di persone a rimanere in movimento, offrendo al contempo supporto clinico ed economico ai chirurghi e al sistema sanitario. I nostri team creano soluzioni per la ricostruzione delle articolazioni, traumi e cranio maxillofacciale, medicina sportiva, estremità, piede e caviglia, colonna vertebrale, robotica e chirurgia digitale.


Il tuo talento aiuterà i pazienti nel loro viaggio verso il benessere. Per ulteriori informazioni, visitare il sito Web https://www.jnj.com/medtech

**Stiamo cercando il miglior talento nel ruolo di Product Specialist Biosurgery.**

**Scopo:** Il Product Specialist sarà responsabile del processo di vendita dei prodotti di riferimento, attraverso la costante interazione con chirurghi e altri professionisti di sala operatoria e supporterà il team nell'esecuzione delle attività per garantire accuratezza, tempestività e qualità dei risultati.


Contribuirà all'implementazione della strategia commerciale e coordinerà dimostrazioni di prodotto e presentazioni cliniche per conferenze e simposi per le regioni: Marche e Umbria

**Responsabilità:**

* Gestire il processo di vendita dei prodotti di riferimento Biosurgery, attraverso una costante interazione con chirurghi e professionisti di sala operatoria.
* Fornire supporto clinico e procedurale durante l’utilizzo dei prodotti, garantendo elevati standard di qualità e affidabilità.
* Supportare il team di Clinical Sales – Surgeons nell’esecuzione delle attività, assicurando accuratezza, tempestività e qualità dei risultati.
* Contribuire allo sviluppo e all’implementazione dei piani operativi dell’area, identificando e supportando la chiusura delle principali opportunità.
* Coordinare e realizzare dimostrazioni di prodotto e presentazioni cliniche in occasione di eventi, congressi e simposi.
* Mantenere un’elevata conoscenza di prodotto, delle best practice e delle soluzioni tecniche, al fine di rispondere efficacemente alle esigenze dei clienti.
* Analizzare trend di mercato e dati commerciali per supportare strategie di crescita e miglioramento della customer retention.
* Agire in linea con i Leadership Imperatives e il Credo Johnson \& Johnson.

**Requisiti:**

* Laurea in discipline scientifiche idonea allo svolgimento dell’attività di Informatore Scientifico del Farmaco (ISF).
* Hai inoltre arricchito il tuo profilo con un Master in Digital Marketing e/o Project Management e/o Business Data Analytics, oppure hai maturato una solida esperienza pratica in uno o più di questi ambiti.
* Esperienza in ruoli commerciali nel settore medicale; l’esperienza nel business Surgery è considerata preferenziale.
* Buona conoscenza del pacchetto Microsoft Office, in particolare Excel; la conoscenza di Power BI è considerata un plus.
* Ottime capacità relazionali, comunicative e di gestione del cliente.
* Italiano madrelingua; inglese fluente.
* Residenza o domicilio coerente con il territorio assegnato (Marche).

**Required Skills:**

**Preferred Skills:**


Business Development, Communication, Customer Centricity, Customer Retentions, Execution Focus, Healthcare Trends, Market Knowledge, Market Research, Presentation Design, Problem Solving, Sales, Solutions Selling, Stakeholder Engagement, Strategic Sales Planning, Sustainable Procurement, Team Management, Vendor Selection
company_addresses: 1 Johnson And Johnson Plaza New Brunswick New Jersey, US  08933
company_description: Caring for the world, one person at a time... inspires and unites the people of Johnson & Johnson.

title: Jr. Planner Specialist
description:
**Genelife S.r.l.,** azienda leader nella progettazione, sviluppo e produzione di integratori alimentari e dispositivi medici per conto terzi (CDMO), è alla ricerca di un **Addetto/a alla pianificazione** per unirsi al nostro team.

**Mansioni:**

\- Inserimento ordini di vendita nel piano di produzione sul file Excel;

\- Schedulazione dal piano di produzione in Excel e nel sistema aziendale Infor Blending;

\- Controllo delle giacenze ed esistenze in magazzino dei materiali per la produzione;

\- Generazione degli ordini di produzione e compilazione file report ordini di produzione;

\- Assistenza nella creazione e compilazione del piano di produzione sul GANTT;

\- Comunicazione e collaborazione con l’ufficio acquisti e il customer service per materiali e date di consegna.

**Requisiti:**

* Laurea (conseguita o in corso) in Ingegneria gestionale
* Interesse per il settore degli integratori o dei medical device
* Buona conoscenza del pacchetto Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint)
* Ottime capacità logiche e organizzative
* Proattività e attitudine al lavoro in team

**Cosa offriamo**

* Stage curricolare o extracurricolare con rimborso spese
* Formazione continua in un contesto dinamico e innovativo
* Possibilità di crescita professionale e inserimento in azienda
* Ambiente di lavoro giovane, collaborativo e orientato al risultato

Contratto di lavoro: Tempo pieno, Tirocinio formativo/stage

Sede di lavoro: Di persona
